card.intuition: A script that your browser runs in the background, separate from a web page, opening the door to features that don't need a web page or user interaction.
card.analogy: A personal assistant who lives in the lobby of your building: they can answer the door for you (intercept network requests) when you are asleep (page closed), or go fetch mail and keep it in a safe (caching) for when you wake up.
when_to_use: Building Progressive Web Apps (PWAs) with offline support
gotchas: Service Workers ONLY work over HTTPS (for security reasons, as they can intercept all network traffic)
They cannot access the DOM directly; they must communicate via postMessage

steps.annotation: Script parsed: navigator.serviceWorker.register() called. Browser downloads the SW file.
Installing: install event fires. precache assets in event.waitUntil(cache.addAll(...)).
Installed/Waiting: SW ready but old SW still controls the page. Waits for all tabs to close.
Activating: activate event fires. Clean up old caches here.
Activated: SW controls all clients. fetch, push, sync events now intercepted.
Redundant: replaced by a newer SW or failed to install. Inert — will be garbage collected.

exercise.prompt: Identify the three main lifecycle events of a Service Worker.
exercise.starter_code:
lifecycle = [
    "1. Registration",
    "2. _________ (Downloading assets)",
    "3. _________ (Taking control of the page)"
]
exercise.test_code:
assert "Install" in lifecycle[1]
assert "Activate" in lifecycle[2]
print("PASS")
